sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Lower-limb prosthetic rehabilitation screening in FIFOEMS should identify socket, skin, comfort, alignment, endurance, and falls issues, and decide when specialist prosthetic input is needed.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Skin irritation, socket discomfort, or poor prosthetic fit
- Reduced walking distance, balance, or endurance
- Falls, fear of falls, or trouble on uneven ground
- Pain at residual limb, back, or contralateral limb

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Escalate for wound breakdown, infection, acute ischaemia, major device failure, or inability to transfer safely
- Prosthetic problems often reflect a combination of socket fit, volume change, component setup, and conditioning
- Do not keep pushing a patient through a clearly unsafe prosthetic setup

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Check skin, comfort, weight-bearing tolerance, and immediate mobility safety
- Reduce risk by modifying walking exposure or using backup aids if needed
- Escalate if the patient cannot safely use the prosthesis or has skin compromise

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Assess fit tolerance, gait pattern, step symmetry, endurance, terrain challenges, and residual limb care understanding
- Coordinate prosthetist, medical, and rehab review when device changes or wound issues are needed
- Build strength, balance, and endurance goals around real work and terrain demands

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Prosthetic rehab decisions should be made with the socket and terrain in mind, not just a flat indoor walk
